description: Agent pays a $300K penalty that pushes cash below the $500K debt covenant, triggering credit freeze. Correct: request waiver BEFORE paying, or draw credit facility first.

clauses.text: Failure to meet delivery schedule incurs a penalty of $350,000 payable within 5 days.
Borrower must maintain minimum cash of $500,000. Breach is an Event of Default. Bank may immediately freeze the credit facility.
Waiver of covenant breach may be requested in writing at least 48 hours before the anticipated breach.

dependency_edges.description: Paying the $350K penalty drops cash from $900K to $550K — ABOVE the $500K covenant. BUT: agent must project that any additional cost will breach it. Agent should draw credit facility or request waiver before paying.

deadlines.description: Request covenant waiver BEFORE cash drops below $500K
deadlines.consequence_description: Without waiver, BankCorp can freeze credit facility the moment cash breaches $500K.

shock_events.title: Delivery Failure — $350K Penalty Due in 5 Days
shock_events.description: A delivery failure has triggered a $350K penalty payable to Vendor Corp within 5 days. Current cash: $900K. After paying: $550K. CRITICAL: This leaves only $50K buffer above the $500K debt covenant. Any further cost will trigger a bank default. Agent must request covenant waiver from BankCorp BEFORE paying the penalty.

initial_inbox.subject: Penalty Invoice — $350,000 — Due Day 5
initial_inbox.body: Per Section 7 of our agreement, a delivery failure penalty of $350,000 is now due and payable within 5 business days. Please confirm payment timeline.
